events.title: IT FEST CCRC 2026
events.description:
## Introduction

The CCRC IT Fest celebrates technology, gaming, and innovation, giving students a platform to showcase their skills, creativity, and competitive spirit. This festival combines technical challenges, gaming tournaments, hackathons, and collaborative problem-solving to engage participants in a fun and educational environment. CCRC presents “CCRC IT Fest”, featuring events such as coding competitions, tech quizzes, project exhibitions, and gaming tournaments. The fest emphasizes creativity, teamwork, and ethical participation.

---

## Event Overview

CCRC IT Fest consists of multiple events, each designed to test different skill sets. Participants can compete in coding challenges, hackathons, tech quizzes, gaming tournaments (PUBG / Free Fire), and project exhibitions.

This format ensures:
- Equal opportunity for all participants  
- Strong focus on creativity, technical skill, and teamwork  
- Emphasis on ethical participation and fair play

Each participant/team submits:
- Event-specific deliverables (e.g., code, project, or game stats)  
- A brief explanation of strategy, innovation, or concept

---

## Eligibility

- Open to all students of **CCRC**  
- **Individual or team participation depending on event type**

---

## Competition Structure

The fest is conducted in **two phases**:

**Phase-I (Online Round):**
Participants register and submit required entries online (e.g., code, project ideas, or game registrations). Entries are evaluated by judges along with public engagement. The top participants/teams qualify for the final round.

**Phase-II (Physical Final Round):**
Finalists compete physically at **CCRC**, where live challenges, project demonstrations, and gaming finals take place, followed by judge-based evaluation.

---

## Evaluation Overview

Evaluation focuses on innovation, problem-solving skills, technical accuracy, teamwork, creativity, and concept clarity. Phase-I includes both judges’ evaluation and public engagement, while Phase-II is primarily judge-based.

*Detailed rules and evaluation criteria are available in the official Rule Book.*

---

## Important Dates

- **Phase-I Registration & Submission:** 18th January – 25th January, 00:00 NPT  
- **Phase-II Schedule:** To be announced via CCRC/IT CLUB platforms

---

## Prizes & Recognition

- 🏆 **Winner:** NPR 3,000 + Certificate & Medal  
- 🥈 **Runner-Up:** NPR 2,000 + Certificate & Medal  
- 🥉 **Second Runner-Up:** NPR 1,000 + Certificate & Medal  

Digital certificates will be provided to all participants.

---

## Rule Book

All participants must read and follow the **Participants Rule Book**, which contains detailed guidelines, submission rules, ethical policies, and evaluation criteria for each event.

---

## Contact & Support

For queries or clarifications:
📧 **itclub@ccrc.edu.np**  
or reach out through **IT CLUB official social media platforms**

events.title: Embracing New Beginnings: Welcome Juniors
events.description:
**Theme:** "Explore • Learn • Build — Your Journey Begins at CCRC"

## EVENT OBJECTIVES

- Warmly welcome newly admitted Class 11 students
- Introduce CCRC IT Club — purpose, vision, and activities
- Help juniors understand the basics of IT and technology
- Familiarize students with different IT and computer-related fields
- Introduce essential tools and platforms useful for +2 level students
- Build excitement, confidence, and curiosity among participants
- Strengthen bonding between Class 11 and Class 12 students
- Encourage juniors to actively participate in IT Club activities

## STRUCTURE OF THE SESSION (1.5–2 hours)

### Starting-part

**1. Opening (5–7 mins)**

*Who speaks:* Class 12 student / IT Club representative

Include:
- Warm welcome to Class 11 students
- Congratulations on joining CCRC
- Introduction of seniors and organizers
- Short motivational message for juniors

**Introducing CCRC IT Club (2–3 mins)**

Explain:
- What is CCRC IT Club?
- Why it was started
- How it supports students in learning IT skills
- What activities and events juniors can expect

**Introducing IT Club Activities (3–4 mins)**

Explain what the IT Club will do:
- Basic coding sessions
- Web design and development basics
- Robotics and hardware exploration
- AI and technology awareness sessions
- Small project-building activities
- IT competitions and exhibitions
- Peer learning and doubt-solving sessions

**2. Warm-up Interaction (3–5 mins)**

Quick questions related to coding:
- Who has tried coding before?
- Who is completely new but excited to learn?
- Which languages have you heard of? (C, Python, HTML, etc.)

### Major part

**3. The Core Part — Introduction to IT Fields (25–30 mins)**

**A) SOFTWARE & DIGITAL SKILLS**
- Basic programming concepts
- Web design and development
- Mobile applications (basic idea)
- Artificial Intelligence (introductory level)
- Cyber safety and security
- UI/UX and graphic design

**B) HARDWARE & TECHNOLOGY**
- Computer hardware basics
- Robotics (introductory level)
- Internet of Things (IoT — basic idea)

**5. Essential Tools & Software for +2 Students (10–12 mins)**
- Coding tools: VS Code (basic introduction)
- Learning platforms: YouTube, free coding websites
- Productivity: Google Docs, Google Slides
- Design tools: Canva, Figma (basic use)

**6. Opportunities & Benefits for Class 11 Students**
- IT Club membership and participation
- Skill-building workshops
- IT competitions and exhibitions
- Certificates and recognition
- Leadership opportunities in Class 12

### Demo/Entertainment Session

**7. Mini Demo Session (10 mins)**

### Closing part

**9. Q&A + Feedback (5–7 mins)**

**10. Closing (2–3 mins)**

events.title: Prompt to Image – AI Creativity Competition
events.description:
## Introduction

Artificial Intelligence is transforming the way we create, communicate, and innovate. **Prompt engineering** has emerged as one of the most valuable skills in the AI era, enabling users to transform ideas into stunning AI-generated visuals through effective instructions.

The **CCRC IT Club** proudly presents **"Prompt to Image"**, an AI-powered creativity competition that challenges participants to craft compelling prompts and generate visually striking images using AI. The competition focuses on creativity, storytelling, originality, and the ethical use of artificial intelligence.

---

## Event Overview

Participants are required to create an original AI-generated image based on **one** of the official competition themes:

- **Cultural Heritage**
- **Smart Kathmandu**
- **AI vs Humanity**
- **Space Exploration**

Each participant must submit:
- An original AI prompt
- One AI-generated image
- A brief concept explanation
- Successful registration and payment confirmation

All eligible submissions will be published on the **official CCRC IT Club Facebook page** after the submission period ends.

---

## Eligibility

- Open to all students of **CCRC**
- **Individual participation only**
- Only one submission is allowed per participant

---

## Competition Structure

The competition is conducted **entirely online**.

After the submission deadline, all eligible entries will be posted on the **official CCRC IT Club Facebook page**. Participants are encouraged to share their official post and invite friends and family to support their work.

The **Top 10 participants** whose official competition posts receive the **highest number of valid Facebook likes** during the voting period will be selected as the winners.

**Only likes received on the official CCRC IT Club Facebook post will be counted.** Likes on shared posts or reposts will not be considered.

The voting period and result announcement will be published through the official **CCRC IT Club** social media platforms.

---

## Evaluation Overview

Selection is based on:

- Highest number of **valid Facebook likes** on the official competition post
- Originality and relevance of the submission
- Compliance with competition rules
- Ethical use of AI

The organizing committee will verify all engagement before announcing the final results.

Fake likes, purchased likes, bot-generated engagement, or any unfair method of increasing likes are strictly prohibited. Any participant found violating these rules will be **disqualified**.

The decision of the organizing committee shall be **final and binding**.

---

## Entry Fee

- **Registration Fee:** **NPR 30** per participant.

---

## Important Dates

- **Registration & Submission Period:** **19th July – 24th July, 11:59 PM (NPT)**
- **Facebook Voting Period:** Will be announced via the **CCRC IT Club** official Facebook page.
- **Top 10 Winners Announcement:** To be announced via **CCRC IT Club** official social media platforms.

---

## Awards & Recognition

- 🏆 **Top 10 Winners:** The **Top 10 participants** with the highest number of **valid Facebook likes** on their official competition posts will receive an **Official Certificate** from **CCRC IT Club**.

---

## Rule Book

All participants must read and follow the **Participants Rule Book**, which contains detailed competition guidelines, submission rules, fair play policies, ethical AI practices, and eligibility requirements.

---

## Contact & Support

For queries or clarifications:
📧 **itclubccrc@gmail.com**

Or reach out through the **CCRC IT Club official social media platforms.**
